channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DOGE puts DEI on chopping block with termination of over $370M in education department grants
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency revealed it slashed $370 million of spending toward diversity, equity, and inclusion at the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Swalwell declares 'all crashes are Trump's fault' as he doubles down on plane disaster blame game
item.description: Rep. Eric Swalwell, D-Calif., declared that President Donald Trump has had more plane crashes in his first month in office than any U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The anti-Musk protest movement is expected to ramp up with Congress on recess
item.description: President Donald Trump's billionaire top lieutenant, Elon Musk, is the focus for thousands of Democratic activists launching a massive protest campaign this week.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA air traffic control staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a fatal mid-air collision in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Duffy brings up Clinton while noting SpaceX workers will visit Air Traffic Control System Command Center
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy mentions Hillary Clinton while noting that SpaceX workers will visit the Air Traffic Control System Command Center
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Activist Curtis Sliwa joins race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City mayoral race just got even more crowded. The founder of the Guardian Angels nonprofit, Curtis Sliwa, has officially filed paperwork to run for the city's top job. He also ran for mayor back in 2021 and lost to the current Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: How Presidents Day has evolved from reverence to retail
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Like the other Founding Fathers, George Washington was uneasy about the idea of publicly celebrating his life. He was the first leader of a new republic, not a king. And yet the United States will once again commemorate its first president on Monday, 293 years after he was born.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Area 51: Fact and fiction
item.description: The Groom Lake, Nevada, facility — about 100 miles northwest of Las Vegas — remains highly secretive.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves with dizzying speed on his to-do list. But there are warning signs in his first month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump approaches the first-month mark in his second term, he has moved with dizzying speed and blunt force to reorder American social and political norms and the economy while redefining the U.S. role in the world.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, JOSH BOAK and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Legislative immunity is a privilege in most states. A speeding ticket could change that in Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — When a police officer gave state Sen. Mark Finchem a speeding ticket, the Arizona Republican didn't need to worry about the consequences.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Musk's latest moves to shrink the federal workforce explained
item.description: President Trump and right-hand man Elon Musk have rolled out a series of moves to cull the federal workforce, forging ahead with numerous methods to fire employees that have already sparked legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Memo: Firestorm grows over Trump DOJ’s deal to drop charges against Eric Adams
item.description: The Trump administration, which has been moving like a juggernaut across the political landscape, has hit a land mine. The decision by Trump’s Justice Department to halt the prosecution of New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) has caused uproar.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump officials signal potential changes at NOAA, the weather and climate agency
item.description: Federal workers at the home agency for the National Weather Service are concerned about a potential overhaul by Trump officials focused on cutting government costs.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, Michael Copley, and Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Ellison tells Minnesota schools to follow law if immigration authorities show up
item.description: Responding to the Trump administration’s decision to allow immigration enforcement in schools, Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison on Friday advised district leaders to ensure that they and any federal officials who arrive at schools follow legal protocols.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Trump official considering running for California governor if Kamala Harris enters race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a Trump administration official, said Friday that he would consider entering the 2026 California governor race if former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris joins the growing list of candidates.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Around 14 percent of Minnesotans have filed their 2024 taxes so far
item.description: Two months out from the tax filing deadline, the Minnesota Department of Revenue is already beginning to see a stream of return filings. The state is encouraging people to file electronically and pick the direct deposit option if they’re looking to get their returns quickly.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: University of St. Thomas scrambling after Dept. of Ed cuts millions in funding
item.description: The $6.3 million grant was targeted under President Donald Trump’s effort to cut programs the government considers “diversity, equity and inclusion,” or DEI.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gretchen Brown, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: An open Minnesota Senate seat is enticing to plenty but unclear how many will take plunge
item.description: Minnesota has its first open-seat Senate race since 2006. Many candidates are giving it a look and both parties say they’re well-positioned to win.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: A federal worker tried to take Trump's 'Fork' resignation offer. Here's what happened
item.description: Liz Goggin, a social worker with the Veterans Health Administration, took the offer to resign in exchange for pay and benefits through September. Then she learned her position was exempt.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis City Council postpones proposal to move violence prevention funds to county
item.description: After a lengthy and sometimes heated discussion, the Minneapolis City Council voted Thursday to indefinitely postpone a proposal to have Hennepin County temporarily oversee two of the city’s violence intervention programs.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: What Sen. Tina Smith’s retirement means for Minnesota politics in 2026
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith announced she wouldn’t seek reelection on Thursday, opening a big field on both sides to replace her in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Brian Bakst, Alanna Elder, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: DOJ sues NY Gov. Hochul, AG James over immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
